reviews.review_title: Love it, but one more thing
reviews.review_description: Can you add people walking around with pets and ships floating around the land? Thanks really love this game 👍🏼
Please add stuff like grass that you can place down and please make the building area bigger

But I would like to see more interaction and updates. After awhile, it becomes boring when you’ve done all you can do. New upgrades and features would be a nice enhancement. I second the notion that it would be cool to walk around inside and maybe be able to decorate with more things and personalize it more. Other than that, I enjoy the relaxation this app provides.

It’s good. Very relaxing and fun but there should be people walking around their town. Going about their daily life. Also there aren’t actually floating towns like shown in the video which is sort of a scam. But it’s still good
Fun little game when your bored but at times feels empty.

Found myself enjoying this game a lot, aimlessly playing urban designer for hours. I love zooming at max and positioning myself on some balcony to look over my town and get a sense of the space. It feels like I'm really there. This game has shortcomings though, lots of little things that could make a big difference. I want to have:

- The option to hold the trigger/A button to quickly add and remove elements one layer at time. As it is you have to to press the trigger hundreds of times if you want to fill a larger space.

- Better control over the weather, season, time of day and sun angle, sky and lighting colour, etc.   I want to see my city at sunset or at night with street lights. Right now it's just a depressing dull grey with cloud-cover, same as my city's weather in Nov/Dec. Not very uplifting, I'm using VR to escape.

- The option to point and click on a spot and be transported there at full magnification and to walk around on the ground, or fly with the joystick like in walkabout mini golf. You can do those manually using panning and pinch-zoom, but it's clunky and imprecise. This would greatly help experience your city at the human scale.

- A bit more control over sculpting a space and deciding where to put little trees and benches and doors and so on. I love that it's done procedurally and how it sometimes create tiny cozy spots automatically, but the variety is limited. I find myself longing to create pretty little town squares and long stair cases. I want to put a small statue or fountain here, a cute store front or coffee shop with a couple tables and chair outside there, moss or vines crawling up a large wall, but I can't.

- The possibility to build over ground instead of water.

- That might be a lot to ask, but more than one choice of architectural style for your whole city.

I guess what I'm saying is that this could be a great "charming miniature old European town" builder, but it's unfortunately still a little too blocky and rigid right now.
This game desperately needs mixed reality update for quest 3. It's such a perfect game for MR

reviews.review_title: Such a beautiful and relaxing game but..
reviews.review_description:
This game is so nice being able to create your own unique town with wonderful colours but I wish there were people because it looks empty without and maybe hand tracking could be fun 🤔 otherwise, definitely worth the price !
Ps WISHLIST25 promo code for 25 percent off 🤩🤩

This is so well done. It’s so relaxing. To people unfamiliar w Townscaper it’s not a game really but a building sim but it auto populates where you point and click. It’s hard to explain other than you have nothing to lose buying it (steal at this price imo) and if you don’t like it or get it - refund within 14 days or 2 hours.

For devs:

What id love to see (and be willing to pay for) is:

- modding - if modding is not available then adding some of the mods as native functions

- city sounds, people, nature (select which ones you want to use)

- more color profiles - many more. I’ve seen some brilliantly done modded stuff w darker colours that looked ridiculously good. If we could build and save our own palettes that would be amazing

- weather - the mod w heavy, light rain and snow is perfect

- day/night

- street view walking mod. Populating it with NPCs.

This has the potential to be a top app on quest in my opinion if some of the above were added. It’s the perfect place to escape and hang out.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the love!! 🥰
And also thank you for your suggestions! At this time we're not able to commit to any major changes to the game even though the things you mention would be awesome! That said, letting us know what you would want is the first step to us considering!

reviews.review_description:
I was hesitant to buy this game. From what I'd seen I thought it would be boring. And what can I tell you. It is.

But in a good way.

There is no challenge. No goal. No expectation.

Just you, your cute little grippers, the tiny color balls and the satisfying *plopp* for every bit you build, or the splash for deleting. 

It serves the inner Monk when you try to bring order and symmetry into your structures, despite the somewhat wanky, chaotic base grid. It's fun to chase the birds ;}  It makes you smile when you see what your combination of houses results in.

But I agree with the review of another lady here - this needs more colors, or a palette, day/night mode (imagine this with lights!), and weather (SNOW <3)

Here's to hoping that the devs will maybe add one or another feature of these after all.

Until then, I just keep happily Monk-ing.
I love the game in every way but the Sky and sea level limitations. Pls update it i need more space to build my castle :P
No real point to it. You can’t save, you can’t really do anything but build over and over. Kind of pointless.

I’m sad to write this review, as I love townscaper and have played it extensively on switch, but for some reason my headsets eyes keep de-syncing and the game gets incredibly blurry. It’s like playing cross eyed. Opening the quest menu is totally clear and easy to see, so it’s definitely a game issue, not a headset issue, and it’s kind of unplayable. Every now and then it fades back in and blurs back out, but it’s not at all reliable, hope this gets fixed, it’s a great game beyond that!

reviews.review_title: Love it in my  top 3 for relaxing play
reviews.review_description:
So.. I have many  vr games between steam and oculus.  This is my first large review..

I own this game on both mobile and on steam.. Excellent port game  for VR I actually think the game is better in VR due to being able to move up and around your creations for better control 

I absolutely love this game it is now in my Top 3 for relaxing play just zone out and pop little houses in .

Cheers

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your love and support of Townscaper! We spent a lot of time and care to make sure we got it "right" in transitioning to VR and seeing reviews like yours make us feel like we got at least close! :)

reviews.review_description:
I don’t leave a lot of reviews but this little gem really deserves it. Like others have said, this is a town building sandbox and not a game. It’s like getting a huge crate of building lego parts and a giant room-sized city grid playmat and you just have it. No objective other than build and have fun. I’ve spent hours in my towns already and will go back for more.

If you’re looking for a city-building management style game, look elsewhere. That said, for the low price, it’s so well worth it with plenty of content and extremely intuitive controls.

The graphics are picture postcard perfect with some cool animations and nice little details in the building designs. With little effort, you can create an Italian style village or an island resort or even a sprawling city on metal stilts over the water. And thank goodness, moving around and zooming in & out are super easy.

Google or check Pinterest to see some of the unbelievable towns being created. There are some crazy designs out there. My jaw dropped several times.

And if you’re a budding artist, this app will fast track your understanding of perspective drawing from any point in space. I think even architects would enjoy this app. And because I happen to have one in the family, guess what she’s getting for a Christmas gift. 😂🎁😂

One small improvement that I believe would go a long way would be to add new maps with different grid patterns for city building. For example, I’d love to have a simple square map with straight rows and columns. Or maybe a circular map and grid. Ooh, how about maps the shapes of different continents? Any of these would be a nice extra (minimal programming hopefully) without even having to change any of the building appearances. And yes, I’m a former programmer so I know very well what design and development and testing involve. 😉

👍🏻👍🏻 Anyway, great job DEVs! 👏🏻👏🏻 Hope this is a mega hit for you!! And by chance, do you have a Discord channel?

reviews.review_title: Your Own Whimsical Town in VR (25% off)
reviews.review_description:
Townscaper VR is a virtual reality game that allows players to create and build their own towns in a sandbox environment. It is a relaxing and creative game where players have the freedom to build their towns in any way they desire.

🌟 Pros:

🏙️ Townscaper VR is a fun and relaxing game that allows players to create and build their own towns in a sandbox environment.
🔨 Players can use the Oculus Touch controllers to place and build different types of buildings and structures in a 3D environment.
🎨 The game offers a variety of different building pieces, such as houses, shops, bridges, and other structures, that players can use to create their towns.
🖌️ The game also includes different color palettes and building styles to choose from, allowing players to create a unique and personalized look for their towns.
🌎 The automatic terrain generation creates a natural and realistic landscape for players to build on.
🔧 The game is quick and responsive and even allows for dual-placement with both controllers simultaneously, with each controller able to place it's own color of building.
🔍 The added depth perception with VR allows for seamlessly placing pieces behind other pieces in a way that flat versions of this game cannot, often to their detriment.
🔨 The precision with which you can build your towns is a game changer.
🌅 The game also features a day/night cycle and weather effects, adding to the immersive experience.
🏙️ Players can zoom in and out, and move around their towns to explore and admire their creations.
📷 Players can save their towns and share them with others.
🏙️ It is a fun and relaxing game that allows players to get lost in their own creative worlds and build towns with no objective other than to have fun.
🎨 The game is worth the low price with plenty of content and extremely intuitive controls.
🔨 The graphics are picture postcard perfect with some cool animations and nice little details in the building designs.
🔍 With little effort, you can create an Italian style village or an island resort or even a sprawling city on metal stilts over the water.
🏙️ Moving around and zooming in & out are super easy.
🎨 For budding artists, this app will fast track your understanding of perspective drawing from any point in space.
🏙️ If you're an architect, you would enjoy this app.
🌅 The game is extremely relaxing and is a great way to escape and unwind.
💰 The game is a steal at its current price, and players have nothing to lose by buying it as they can refund it within 14 days or 2 hours if they don't like it.
🔨 The game is simple and easy to use, with no tutorial needed.
🔍 There are pleasant surprises as you go along, like clothes lines that pop up between homes and bee boxes, stone walls between homes.
🐦 The birds perched on the roofs that fly away is a cute touch.
🎨 The cute colors and whimsical architecture are charming and enjoyable to look at.
🧘‍♀️ The game is perfect for people who like slow-paced, simple, and relaxing games like Minecraft or other creative games.
🔨 The game's interface is fantastically efficient and slick.
🔍 Being able to view those cute buildings and your little town with your natural stereoscopic vision, move around to see the parallax change and being able to scale your view freely in VR is an amazing moment.
🌟Great work by Oskar Stålberg!

🌟 Cons:

🌎 The game could benefit from adding new maps with different grid patterns for city building.
🌟 Some players have suggested that there could be more content added to the game, but it's not by any means bad as it is.
🏠 The game is limited in its building options, allowing only to place plants, benches and etc that will automatically spawn but not able to place furniture wherever you want on the map.
🏙️ Some players have found the game map and max height are small and could be expanded to allow more room for building.
🚶‍♀️ The game lacks people that can walk around your town which would make the game more lively and interactive.
🏙️ The game is limited in that you can only build so far out and so far up. If your town has too much sprawl, you will be limited by how tall your buildings can be. If your town has a lot of tall buildings, you will be limited by how far out your town can reach.
🔨 The PC version of the game offers a much greater amount of space to build and there is no limit for how tall your buildings can be regardless of how much sprawl your town has.

🌟 Overall, Townscaper is a slow-paced, simple, relaxing masterpiece. It's a great game for people who like creative games like Minecraft. The cute colors and whimsical architecture are charming and enjoyable to look at. The developers are not to blame for players' opinions and preferences. I would recommend this game to anyone looking for a fun and relaxing game. The developer Oskar Stålberg has done a great job in creating this game.

If you're interested in Townscaper VR, now it's a great time to get it as it currently has a 25% off coupon link available (https://www.oculus.com /appreferrals/xinwix/4529223267133070/?utm_source=2). I highly recommend giving Townscaper VR a try, especially for those who are looking for a relaxing and stress-free experience. It's a great game for players who enjoy building and creating, and for those who are looking for a unique and personalized experience. Overall, Townscaper VR is a fantastic game that is well worth the investment.

https://www.oculus.com /appreferrals/xinwix/4529223267133070/?utm_source=2
A 100% waste. Limited, boring, and contained. Your only options is color basically.
I want my money back..this ain't a town...you make a few blocks?!? WTF developer please tell me what to do besides go to Russia for help?!?
It’s just so relaxing and calm unlike other vr games and I love making combinations of the houses but please add more and I love not buying the houses with money

I thought there would be a limit to what I could create, and it would all look the same, but i was wrong. Each of the towns I’ve created look completely different.  I’ve even made a few castles. It’s so simple ro use (no tutorial..you don’t need one). There’s pleasant surprises as you go along. Like a clothes line that pops up between two homes to bee boxes, stone walls betwwen homes. It’s hard to explain, so just try it!  The birds perched on the roofs that fly away is a cute touch. I’d love to see people walking around.
